doc_cf657755.content:
New functionality for approving product addition requests
As a system administrator, I want to be able to approve user requests for adding products to the system.
Product addition request management screen
The application menu must implement the ability to work with product addition requests:
This menu is available only to a user with the Superadmin role
The approval page includes products created by users that have not yet been approved
A table with approval requests that includes the following fields:
Product name
Product description
Product category
Price
For each product, an “Approve” or “Reject” button is present.
When a product is approved, in the products_service DB, in the products table, the value of the is_available field changes from False to True
When a request is rejected, the product is deleted from the DB.

Technical implementation
When a product is created in the products_service MS, the product is initially created with is_available=False.
Automatically after a product is created, a topic is created in Kafka that contains the product id:
Example record:
{"product_id": "8f8c7a2b-ac8e-4c4f-84e2-b4ae0a340075"}
The message will be automatically removed from Kafka
When navigating to the request approval page, the auth (authorization) microservice reads all records from the “product_topic” topic, requests product information by id, and displays it to the superadmin in the interface.
The NoSQL database Redis is used to aggregate requests from Kafka.
When a request is rejected or approved, the request must be removed from Redis.

doc_373fa7e2.content:
Technical requirements for the Products_service microservice
Managing products, suppliers, and warehouses in the system
Description:
As a user, I want to be able to manage products, suppliers, and warehouses, to add, edit, view, and delete records in these categories.
Access to this data is performed via an authorization token with access rights differentiation for authorized users.
1. Product management
1.1 Creating a product
• The user must be able to create a new product.
• A product with the same name cannot be created; a 422 This product is already existed error must be returned.
• By default, the product is created in the DB with the is_available field set to False

Data that the user must provide:
Name:
• Required field.
• Length from 3 to 100 characters.
• Only letters and digits
Description:
• Optional field.
• Maximum 500 characters (no restrictions on the characters used).
Category:
• Optional field.
• Maximum 50 characters.
• Only letters and digits
Price:
• Required field.
• Positive number with 2 digits after the decimal point, greater than 0, maximum 9999999.99 (input format: 20.00)
Product quantity:
• Required field
• Integer, not less than 0
Constraints:
• Must be an integer
• The value cannot be less than 0
Supplier:
• Required field.
• Supplier ID from the supplier table
Constraints:
• Must be a valid UUID
• The ID must correspond to an already existing supplier in the database
Product images
• Formats: png, jpeg, jpg
• Optional field
• Maximum length: 255 characters
• Stored in the DB as an image url
Product weight (weight)
• Optional field
• Positive number with a decimal point
Constraints:
• If specified, the value must be greater than 0
• Maximum 6 digits (including 2 digits after the decimal point)
Product dimensions (dimensions)
• Optional field
• Maximum length: 100 characters
• Allowed input: integer digits and the symbol x
Product manufacturer (manufacturer) — usually refers to the name of the company that produces the product
• Optional field
• Maximum length: 100 characters
• Only letters and digits
User ID
Automatically filled with the ID of the user who created the product
1.2 Getting the list of products
• The user must be able to get the list of all products in the system, regardless of who created the product.
• Returned fields: name, description, category, price, quantity in stock, supplier, weight, dimensions, manufacturer.
• If the current user is not authorized, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.
• If there are no products, an empty list is returned
1.3 Getting a product by ID
• The user must be able to get information about a specific product by its ID.
• If the product with the specified ID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
• If the current user is not authorized, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.
1.4 Updating a product
• The user must be able to update the product data by its ID.
• Fields available for update:
- Name, description, category, price, quantity in stock, supplier, image (url), weight, dimensions, manufacturer.
• If the product with the specified ID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
• If the current user is not authorized, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.
1.5 Deleting a product
• The user must be able to delete a product by its ID.
• If the product with the specified ID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
• If the current user is not authorized, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.

Requirements for access:
• The user must be authorized.
• Bearer token (JWT) is passed via the Authorization header.

2. Supplier management
2.1 Creating a supplier
• The user must be able to create a new supplier.
• A supplier with the same name cannot be created; a 422 This supplier is already existed error must be returned.

Data that the user must provide to create a new supplier:
Name:
• Required field.
• Length from 3 to 100 characters.
Supplier contact person (contact_name)
• Required field
• Maximum length: 100 characters
Contact person’s email (contact_email)
• Required field
• 20 characters, an @ sign, valid email format
Phone number (phone_number)
• Optional field
• Maximum length: 15 characters, includes only integer digits and the + symbol
Supplier address (address)
• Optional field
• Maximum length: 200 characters, only letters and digits
Supplier country (country)
• Optional field
• Maximum length: 50 characters, letters only
Supplier city (city)
• Optional field
• Maximum length: 50 characters, letters only, a space or hyphen is allowed
Supplier website (website)
• Optional field
• Must have URL validation
2.2 Getting the list of suppliers
• The user must be able to get the list of all suppliers.
• Returned fields:
- Supplier ID.
- Name.
- Contact person.
- Contact email.
- Phone.
- Address.
- Country and city.
- Website.
• If there are no suppliers in the DB, it returns an empty list
2.3 Getting a supplier by ID
• The user must be able to get information about a specific supplier by its ID.
• If the supplier with the specified ID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
• If the current user is not authorized, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.
2.4 Updating a supplier
• The user must be able to update the supplier data by its ID.
• Fields available for update:
- Name, contact person, email, phone, address, country, city, website.
• If the supplier with the specified ID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
• If the current user is not authorized, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.
2.5 Deleting a supplier
• The user must be able to delete a supplier by its ID.
• If the supplier with the specified ID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
• If the current user is not authorized, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.
• A supplier cannot be deleted (an error must be returned) if this supplier already has products linked to them in the system

Requirements for access:
• The user must be authorized.
• Bearer token (JWT) is passed via the Authorization header.

3. Warehouse management
3.1 Creating a warehouse
• The user must be able to create a new warehouse.
• A warehouse with the same location cannot be created; there must be a 422 error

Data that the user must provide:
Warehouse location (location)
• Required field
• Length: maximum 255 characters (no restrictions on the entered values)
Warehouse manager’s first name and last name (manager_name)
• Optional field
• Maximum length: 100 characters (letters only)
Warehouse capacity (capacity)
• Required field
Constraints:
• The field must be a positive number showing the maximum volume in cubic meters that can be stored in the warehouse
Current quantity of products in the warehouse (current_stock)
• Automatically filled with the value 0
Constraints:
• The field must be an integer not less than 0, reflecting the current quantity of products in the warehouse
Warehouse phone number (contact_number)
• Optional field
• Maximum length: 15 characters, includes only integer digits and the + symbol
Warehouse contact email (email)
• Optional field
• Maximum length: 255 characters. Must have email validation similar to the login field
Warehouse activity (is_active)
• Data type: boolean
• Required field
Warehouse area (area_size)
• Data type: floating-point number
• Maximum value 1000000.00
• Optional field
• The value must be a positive number representing the warehouse area in square meters (input format: 1.25)
Warehouse creation date (created_at)
• Data type: date and time
• Automatically generated field. The warehouse creation date is automatically assigned when added to the DB and is not available for user editing

3.2 Getting a warehouse by ID
• The user must be able to get information about a specific warehouse by its ID.
• If the warehouse with the specified ID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
• If the current user is not authorized, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.

3.3 Updating a warehouse
• The user must be able to update the warehouse data by its ID.
• Fields available for update:
- Location, manager’s name, capacity, current stock, phone, email, area.
• If the warehouse with the specified ID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
• If the current user is not authorized, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.

3.4 Deleting a warehouse
• The user must be able to delete a warehouse by its ID.
• If the warehouse with the specified ID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
• If the current user is not authorized, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.
• A warehouse cannot be deleted (an error must be returned) if this warehouse already has products

Requirements for access:
• The user must be authorized.
• Bearer token (JWT) is passed via the Authorization header.

4. Managing products in warehouses
As a user, I want to be able to add, update, and delete products located in a warehouse. I also want to get the list of all products in a specific warehouse in order to effectively manage stock and track product availability.
4.1 Getting the list of products in a warehouse
Description: The user can get the list of all products located in the specified warehouse.
Parameters:
• warehouse_id: warehouse UUID.
Criteria:
• The user must be authorized.
• On a successful request, a list of products located in the warehouse is returned, containing:
• product_id: product UUID.
• quantity: quantity of products in the warehouse (integer, not less than 0).
• If the warehouse ID is incorrect, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
4.2 Adding products to a warehouse
Description: The user can add a new product to the specified warehouse by providing its ID and quantity.
Parameters:
• warehouse_id: UUID of the warehouse to which the product is being added.
• product_id: UUID of the product being added to the warehouse.
• quantity: quantity of products to be added (integer, not less than 0).
Criteria:
• The user must be authorized.
• On a successful request, the product is added to the warehouse and a 201 Created code is returned:
• product_warehouse_id: UUID of the product-warehouse link.
• product_id: UUID of the added product.
• warehouse_id: UUID of the warehouse to which the product was added.
• quantity: quantity of the added product.
• If there is no authorization, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.
• If a warehouse or product with the specified UUIDs does not exist, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
4.3 Updating the quantity of products in a warehouse
Description: The user can update the quantity of an already existing product in a warehouse.
Parameters:
• product_warehouse_id: UUID of the product-warehouse link.
• quantity: new quantity of products (integer, not less than 0).
Criteria:
• On a successful request, the quantity of products in the warehouse is updated, and the updated data is returned:
• product_id: product UUID.
• warehouse_id: warehouse UUID.
• quantity: updated quantity of products.
• If there is no authorization, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.
• If the product-warehouse link with the specified UUID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
4.4 Removing products from a warehouse
Description: The user can remove a product from the specified warehouse.
Parameters:
• product_warehouse_id: UUID of the product-warehouse link.
Criteria:
• On a successful request, the product_warehouse_id record and its data are completely deleted.
• If there is no authorization, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.
• If the product-warehouse link with the specified UUID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.


Technical and security requirements:
• All requests must contain a valid JWT token in the Authorization header for identification and authorization.

doc_9889f9ce.content:
Integration requirements
Documentation for Kafka topics and message formats in the current implementation

1. Kafka topics in use
In the current implementation of the orders microservice (order_service), the following Kafka topics are used:

1. orders: a topic to which messages about the creation of new orders are sent. This topic is used to notify other microservices that a new order has been created.
2. order_responses: a topic from which the orders microservice receives responses from other services about the state of orders, such as confirmation or rejection of an order.

2. Message formats
All messages are transmitted in JSON format and contain specific fields depending on the event type.

2.1 Messages sent to the orders topic
OrderCreated event
Description: Sent when a new order is created. Contains information about the order and the items in it.

Message format:
{
  "event_type": "OrderCreated",
  "order_id": "UUID",
  "user_id": "UUID",
  "order_items": [
    {
      "product_id": "UUID",
      “warehouse_id": "UUID",
      "quantity": "int"
    }
  ],
  "timestamp": "ISO8601 datetime"
}

Fields:
• event_type: the event type (in this case "OrderCreated").
• order_id: unique order identifier.
• user_id: identifier of the user who created the order.
• order_items: list of items in the order.
• product_id: product identifier.
• warehouse_id: warehouse identifier
• quantity: quantity of the product.
• timestamp: time of event creation in ISO8601 format.

2.2 Messages received from the order_responses topic
The orders microservice subscribes to the order_responses topic and processes the following types of messages:
OrderConfirmed event
Description: The received message means that the order has been confirmed by another service (for example, the stock management service).
Message format:

{
  "event_type": "OrderConfirmed",
  "order_id": "UUID",
  "confirmed_at": "ISO8601 datetime"
}

Fields:
• event_type: "OrderConfirmed".
• order_id: identifier of the confirmed order.
• confirmed_at: time of order confirmation in ISO8601 format.

OrderRejected event
Description: The received message means that the order has been rejected by another service for some reason.
Message format:

{
  "event_type": "OrderRejected",
  "order_id": "UUID",
  "reason": "string",
  "rejected_at": "ISO8601 datetime"
}

Fields:
• event_type: "OrderRejected".
• order_id: identifier of the rejected order.
• reason: reason for rejecting the order.
• rejected_at: time of order rejection in ISO8601 format.

3. Order processing flow
3.1 Creating an order
• The user sends a request to create an order via the GraphQL API.
• The orders microservice creates an order record in the database with the pending status.
• An OrderCreated message is sent to the orders topic to notify other services about the new order.

3.2 Order processing by other services
• Other microservices (for example, the stock or products management service) subscribe to the orders topic.
• Having received the OrderCreated message, they check the availability and sufficient quantity of the items in the warehouse where the item is located.
• If all items are available:
• They reserve the items and write off the received quantity.
• They send an OrderConfirmed message to the order_responses topic.
• If there are not enough items or other problems arise:
• They send an OrderRejected message to the order_responses topic with the reason specified.

3.3 Updating the order status
• The orders microservice subscribes to the order_responses topic.
• Having received an OrderConfirmed or OrderRejected message, it updates the order status in the database:
On OrderConfirmed:
• The order status is updated to completed.
• The updated_at field is updated to the current time.
On OrderRejected:
• The order status is updated to cancelled.
• The updated_at field is updated to the current time.
• The reason for order rejection is logged.

doc_284684db.content:
UI requirements for the product management tab
1. Product management screen
Goal: To provide the user with the ability to add, view, and manage products in the system.
Requirements:
• Page title: “Product management”.
• “Add new product” button
• List of current products created by any user
• Only the list of products that have been approved by the admin is displayed, that is, products with is_available = True
• Opens a modal window for creating a new product or for editing.
• A product search form by name must be implemented. The search works via the “Find” button, and the found product must be displayed in the list.
2. Modal window for adding a new product
Goal: To allow the user to create a new product by specifying the required data.
Requirements:
• Modal window title: “Add new product”.
• Form fields:
Product name:
• The field is required.
Description:
• The field is optional.
Category:
• The field is optional.
Price:
• The field is required.
Product quantity (how much of the product is in the delivery):
• The field is required.
Supplier:
• Drop-down list with suppliers loaded from the database.
• The field is required.
Product images
• Image upload in formats: png, jpeg, jpg
• The field is optional.
Product weight (weight)
• The field is optional.
Product dimensions (dimensions)
• The field is optional.
Product manufacturer (manufacturer) — usually indicates the name of the company that produces the product
• The field is optional.

“Create product” button:
• Submits the form to create a new product.
• On successful creation of the product, it closes the modal window, clears the form, and refreshes the product list.

The requirements for filling in the fields are specified in the technical documentation. If a field is filled in incorrectly, validation for this field must be triggered and the user must see a message about the incorrect input.
3. Product list table
Goal: To display existing products with the ability to edit and delete them.
Requirements:
• Table title: “Product list”.
Table columns:
• Product ID.
• Name.
• Description.
• Category.
• Price.
• Actions: contains buttons for editing and deleting the product.
When the product name is clicked, a pop-up window must open that contains all the fields about the product from item 2 of these requirements.

4. Edit and delete functionality
Goal: To provide the ability to edit product information and delete it.
Requirements:
“Edit” button:
• Opens the editing form (modal window) for the selected product.
“Delete” button:
• Requests confirmation of deletion.
• Deletes the product from the database upon confirmation.
• Refreshes the product list after successful deletion.

doc_4dd07e58.content:
UI requirements for the warehouse management tab
1. Warehouse management screen
Goal: To provide the user with the ability to add, view, and manage warehouse information.
Requirements:
• Page title: “Warehouse management”.
• “Add new warehouse” button
• List of current warehouses created by any user
• Opens a modal window for creating a new warehouse or for editing.

2. Modal window for adding a new warehouse
Goal: To provide input and saving of data about a new warehouse in the system.
Requirements:
• Form fields:
Location:
• The field is required.
Manager’s name:
• The field is optional.
Capacity (cu. m):
• The field is required.
Current total quantity of all items:
• Automatically filled based on the quantity of products in the warehouse; default is 0.
Contact phone:
• The field is optional.
Contact email:
• The field is optional.
Activity:
• Drop-down list with “Active” and “Inactive” options.
• The field is required.
Area (sq. m):
• The field is optional.

“Create warehouse” button:
• Submits the form, adds a new warehouse, clears the fields after a successful addition, and refreshes the warehouse list.

3. Warehouse list table
Goal: To display existing warehouses with information and the ability to edit or delete them.
Requirements:
• Table title: “Warehouse list”.
Table columns:
• Location.
• Manager.
• Capacity.
• Current stock (current total number of product items in the warehouse).
• Activity (displayed as “Active” or “Inactive”).
• Area (sq. m).
• Actions: contains buttons for editing and deletion.

When the “View” button is clicked, a pop-up window must open that contains all the fields about the warehouse from item 2 of these requirements.
This window must contain a “Product information” button, which, when clicked, must open a page of the specific warehouse in a new tab that contains information about the products (the page description is split off into a separate requirement).
4. Edit and delete functionality
Goal: To provide the user with the ability to update warehouse information and delete a warehouse.
Requirements:
“Edit” button:
• Opens the editing form (modal window) for the selected warehouse.
“Delete” button:
• Requests confirmation of deletion.
• Deletes the warehouse from the database upon confirmation.
• Refreshes the warehouse list after successful deletion.

doc_972c9a9d.content:
UI requirements for the supplier management tab
1. Supplier management screen
Goal: To provide the user with the ability to add, view, and manage supplier data.
Requirements:
• Page title: “Supplier management”.
• “Add new supplier” button:
• Visible on the screen, placed above the supplier list table.
• Opens a modal window for creating a new supplier.
• The “Create supplier” button submits the form data and adds a new supplier to the database.
• A supplier search form by name must be implemented. The search works via the “Find” button, and the found supplier must be displayed in the list.
2. Modal window for adding a new supplier
Goal: To provide input and saving of a new supplier’s data in the system.
Requirements:
• Form fields:
Supplier name:
• The field is required.
Contact person:
• The field is required.
Email:
• The field is required.
• Validation with an error message in case of incorrect format.
Phone:
• The field is optional.
Address:
• The field is optional.
Country:
• The field is optional.
City:
• The field is optional.
Website:
• The field is optional.
The requirements for filling in the fields are specified in the technical documentation. If a field is filled in incorrectly, validation for this field must be triggered and the user must see a message about the incorrect input.

“Create supplier” button:
• Submits the form, adds a new supplier, clears the fields after a successful addition, and refreshes the supplier list.

3. Supplier list table
Goal: To display the list of suppliers with information and the ability to edit or delete them.
Requirements:
• Table title: “Supplier list”.
• Table columns:
• Name.
• Contact person.
• Email.
• Phone.
• Address.
• Country.
• City.
• Website.
• Actions: contains buttons for editing and deletion.

4. Edit and delete functionality
Goal: To provide the user with the ability to update supplier information and delete suppliers.
Requirements:
“Edit” button:
• Opens the form (modal window) for editing the selected supplier.
“Delete” button:
• Requests confirmation of deletion.
• Deletes the supplier from the database upon confirmation.
• Automatically refreshes the list after successful deletion.

doc_3b652be4.content:
UI requirements for a specific warehouse page
General information about the warehouse
Goal: To provide the user with information about the warehouse and the ability to manage the products stored in this warehouse.
Page title: Displays the warehouse location.
The page must contain information about the warehouse:
Manager’s first name and last name
Capacity
Current total quantity of all items
Contact phone
Contact email
Activity
Area (sq. m)

“Add product to warehouse” button:
• Opens the form for adding a new product to the warehouse.
The form includes the following fields:
• Product ID (entering the ID manually).
• Product quantity (a positive integer).
On successful addition of the product, displays the message “Product successfully added to warehouse”.

List of products in the warehouse
Goal: To allow the user to view the list of products in the warehouse and manage their quantity. A paginated list of products (20 products displayed per page)
Requirements
Product table columns:
• Product ID.
• Product name.
• Quantity in the warehouse.
• Actions (edit and delete).

“Edit” (Update) button:
• Opens the form for changing the quantity of the product in the warehouse.
• Field for updating the product quantity:
• Constraint: positive integer.
• On successful update of the quantity, displays the notification “Product quantity successfully updated”.

• “Delete” button:
• Requests confirmation of removing the product from the warehouse.
• Upon confirmation, removes the product from the warehouse and refreshes the list.
• Displays the notification “Product successfully removed from warehouse” after a successful removal.

doc_c4af9983.content:
UI requirements for the microservice
UI requirements for the user registration and management system
1. User registration screen
Goal: To provide the user with the ability to register by specifying the required data.
Requirements:
Registration form:
Name input field:
• Placeholder: “User name”.
• Constraint: minimum 3 characters, maximum 50 characters.
• Validation: display an error message if fewer than 3 or more than 50 characters are entered.
Email input field:
• Placeholder: “Email”.
• Constraint: valid email format.
• Validation: display an error message if the email does not match the standard format.
• When attempting to register with an already existing email, the message “This email is already registered” must be displayed.
Password input field:
• Placeholder: “Password”.
• Constraint: minimum 8 characters.
• Validation: display an error message if the password is shorter than 8 characters.
• The password must be masked (symbols instead of characters).
“Register” button.
• On successful registration, the user must see a successful registration message and be redirected to the login page.
Notes:
• All fields are required.
• The form must be styled with support for mobile devices (responsive design).

2. Login (sign-in) screen
Goal: To provide the user with the ability to log in to gain access to the system.
Requirements:
Login form:
Email input field:
• Placeholder: “Email”.
• Constraint: valid email.
Password input field:
• Placeholder: “Password”.
• The password is masked (symbols instead of characters).
“Sign in” button.
• If the email or password is incorrect, the error message “Incorrect email or password” must be displayed.
• After successful login, the user gains access to the system.
Notes:
• Add a “remember me” option for saving the authorization token.
• Add a button for navigating to the registration screen (“No account? Sign up”).

3. User list screen (for super-admin)
Goal: To provide the super-admin with access to information about all users and the ability to manage their roles.
Requirements:
Users table:
Columns:
• ID (UUID).
• Name.
• Email.
• Role (super-admin/regular user).
• “Promote to super-admin” button in the row of every user who is not yet a super-admin.
• Search by name or email in the user list.
• If the user is a super-admin, the promotion button is not displayed.

Notes:
• For a large number of users, the table must support pagination (after 10 records).
• A filter by status (super-admin/regular user) is available.

4. Notifications and error messages
Goal: To provide the user with feedback at every stage of interaction with the system.
Requirements:
• All notifications and error messages must be displayed in a user-friendly form:
• Successful registration: a green notification with the text “Registration was successful”.
• Error during registration or login: a red notification with text corresponding to the error (for example, “Incorrect email or password”, “This email is already registered”).
• Successful rights elevation: a green notification “User successfully promoted to super-admin”.
• Error during rights elevation: a red notification with the error text.
Notes:
• Notifications and error messages must state the exact reasons for the error, if they are known.

5. General requirements
• Responsive design: All pages must display correctly on both desktop computers and mobile devices.
• Security: Everywhere passwords are entered, they must be masked.
• Cross-browser support: The interface must support the latest versions of all major browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge).

doc_c7db70f7.content:
UI requirements for the orders microservice
The UI of the order management microservice represents a separate tab of the application that allows creating orders for stores and customers from products in accordance with the items available in the warehouse. It provides the functionality of an online store, including viewing, searching, and filtering products, as well as creating and managing orders. The implementation separates access rights based on roles:
• Regular users can view the product catalog, add items to the cart, place orders, and manage their orders (view and cancel).
• Administrators have access to all orders, can track their statuses, filter orders by various criteria, and change their state.

Main UI features:
1. Product catalog: display of products with complete information (name, image, description, supplier, price, stock availability).
2. Cart: managing added products, changing quantities, and placing an order.
3. Personal account: user access to the list of their orders with the ability to cancel.
4. Admin panel: interface for managing all orders in the system with the ability to update their statuses.
1. Main page (product catalog)
Goal: To provide users with a convenient interface for viewing, searching, and filtering products.
Only products that are assigned to a specific warehouse are displayed.
A drop-down list of warehouses where the required warehouse must be selected, after which items available for ordering in this warehouse will be displayed.
1. Page header:
• Product search field.
• Placeholder: “Find a product”.
• Features: search by name.
• “Cart” buttons
2. Product list (cards):
Displayed information:
• Product name.
• Image.
• Description (short text, maximum 200 characters).
• Supplier.
• Price.
• Warehouse (location).

Actions for the user:
• “Add to cart” button.
• If the item is out of stock, the button is inactive with the text: “Out of stock”.
3. Filters:
• Price range (slider).
• Stock availability (only items in stock).

2. Cart
Goal: To provide the ability to place a shipment order.
1. Product list:
Displayed data:
• Product name.
• Quantity.
• Unit price and total cost.
• Increase/decrease the quantity (not more than available in the warehouse).
• Remove the item from the cart.
2. Total order cost.
3. “Place order” button:
• Error message if there are not enough items in the warehouse.

3. Employee personal account (My shipments)
Goal: To provide users with access to their shipment orders.
1. Order list:
Displayed data:
• Order number (order_id).
• Status (pending, completed, cancelled).
• Creation date and time.
• List of items with quantity and cost.
Features:
• “Cancel order” button (only for orders with the pending status).

4. Admin panel
Goal: To provide administrators with access to managing shipment orders.
Administrator is a single account for both the warehouse management service and the order management service.
1. List of all orders:
• Filters by status and creation date
• Displayed data is similar to the user’s personal account.
2. Viewing order details:
• Ability to change the order status.

doc_f121b665.content:
UI requirements for the chats microservice
The UI of the chat management microservice represents a separate tab of the application that provides messaging functionality between users of the system. It allows creating and managing chats, sending and receiving messages in real time, and managing chat participants. The implementation separates access rights based on roles:
• Regular users can view chats available to them, send and receive messages, and view conversation history.
• Administrators (superadmins) can additionally create new chats and add participants to existing chats.
Main UI features:
Chat list.
Goal: To provide users with quick access to available chats and the ability to switch between them.
Displayed data:
Chat name.
“Add participants” button (available only to administrators).
Actions:
Selecting a chat to view messages and send new ones.
Adding participants to the selected chat (for administrators).
Messages window.
Goal: To provide real-time messaging between chat participants.
Displayed data:
Messages split by sender (aligned to the left for incoming, to the right for outgoing).
Sender name and timestamp for each message.
Actions:
Entering message text (up to 1000 characters).
“Send” button to send a message.
“Show history” button to load the entire previous conversation.
Creating a new chat (administrators only)
Goal: To provide administrators with the ability to create new chats and define participants.
Actions:
“Create new chat” button opens a modal window.
In the modal window it is necessary to specify:
Chat name (100-character limit).
Select participants from the list of system users (the administrator is added automatically).
Specify whether the chat is a group chat (when selecting a group chat, there is the ability to add more than one user through the UI).
“Create” button sends a request to create a new chat.
Adding participants to an existing chat (administrators only)
Goal: To provide administrators with the ability to add new participants to already existing chats.
Actions:
“Add participants” button next to the chat name opens a modal window.
In the modal window, only users who are not yet participants of this chat are displayed.
The ability to select several users and add them to the chat.

Technical details:
• A WebSocket connection is used for real-time messaging.
• User name caching is implemented to minimize requests to the server.
• Messages retain their order when displayed.

doc_5019aa4f.content:
General business requirements
User Story 1: New user registration
As a new user
I want to register in the system
So that I can start using the warehouse management application
Acceptance Criteria:
The registration page must contain fields for entering name, email, and password.
When an invalid email or a short password (less than 8 characters) is entered, the system must display an error message.
After successful registration, the user receives a confirmation email at the specified e-mail.
The user must be automatically authenticated after confirming the e-mail.

User Story 2: User authorization
As a registered user
I want to log into the system
So that I can access warehouse management and my data
Acceptance Criteria:
The login page must contain fields for entering e-mail and password.
On successful login, the user must be redirected to the main page of the application.
If incorrect data is entered (wrong e-mail or password), the user must receive an error message.
There must be a “forgot password” mechanism for recovering access.

User Story 3: Adding a product to the warehouse
As a user
I want to add a new product to the warehouse
So that I can manage its stock and use it for orders
Acceptance Criteria:
The user must be able to specify the product name, description, and quantity in stock.
When a negative quantity or an empty name is entered, the system must display an error message.
After a product is added, it must be displayed in the product list.
Each product must be assigned a unique identifier (product_id).

User Story 4: Viewing the product list
As a user
I want to view the list of all products in the warehouse
So that I can see their quantity and other important information
Acceptance Criteria:
The product list must display the name, quantity, and a brief description.
The user can filter products by name or quantity.
The user can see products only within their own data (access restriction).
If there are no products, the system must display a message about an empty warehouse.

User Story 5: Editing product information
As a user
I want to edit product information
So that I can update its description or change the quantity in stock
Acceptance Criteria:
The user can change the product name, description, or quantity.
The system must not allow entering a negative quantity or empty values.
After successful editing, the updated information must be displayed in the product list.
On an unsuccessful update, the system must display an error message.

User Story 6: Creating an order
As a user
I want to create an order for products from the warehouse
So that I can place a request to receive them
Acceptance Criteria:
The user can select products from the list and specify a quantity for each product in the order.
If there is not enough stock to fulfill the order, the system must warn about it.
After an order is successfully created, it must appear in the order list with the current status “pending”.
The order is assigned a unique identifier (order_id), and it records the user who created it.

User Story 7: Viewing and managing orders
As a user
I want to see the list of my orders
So that I can track their status and cancel them if necessary
Acceptance Criteria:
The user must see a list of all their orders with information about the products and quantities.
Order statuses: “pending”, “completed”, “canceled”.
The user can cancel an order if it has not yet been completed.
After an order is canceled, its status must change to “canceled”, and the products must be returned to stock.

User Story 8: Logging user actions
As a system administrator
I want to be able to view logs of user actions
So that I can track all changes made to the system
Acceptance Criteria:
Every user action must be recorded in the logs (for example, product creation, editing, order creation, etc.).
Logs must include information about the date and time of the action, as well as the user identifier.
The administrator can filter logs by action type and date.
Logs are available only to the administrator.

User Story 9: Receiving low-stock notifications
As a user
I want to receive notifications when the quantity of a product in the warehouse falls below a certain level
So that I can replenish stock in a timely manner
Acceptance Criteria:
The user can set a minimum product level at which a notification will be sent to them.
If the product quantity in the warehouse falls below this level, the system must send a notification to the user.
Notifications can be shown in the interface (via websockets) or sent to e-mail.
The user can view the notification history.

doc_f02e1d02.content:
Basic project concept
1. Defining requirements and functionality
First of all, it is necessary to precisely define which functions will be available in the application:
Users: registration, authentication, working with their own data.
Products: adding products to the warehouse, editing, deleting, viewing product information.
Stock: managing product quantities, notifications about low stock levels.
Orders: creating, fulfilling, and canceling orders.
Reports and analytics: reports on product movement, order statuses, and warehouse balances.
Real time: real-time updates via websockets.
2. Application architecture
A microservice architecture can be used to implement this project. This will allow distributing the load and separate tasks across different services, which will simplify scalability and testing.
Microservice 1: Users — manages registration, authentication, and storage of user data.
Microservice 2: Products and warehouses — managing products, adding, deleting, updating product information and their availability in the warehouse.
Microservice 3: Orders — responsible for creating and managing orders, tracking their fulfillment.
Kafka — for sending messages between microservices about order status changes, stock levels, etc.
Logging via the ELK stack — monitoring actions and events in the application.
3. Technology selection
Database:
PostgreSQL for storing information about users, products, orders, and warehouses.
A non-relational DB (for example, Cassandra) for storing transactions, logs, or caching order data.
Backend:
Python (FastAPI or Flask) for building the API and interacting with microservices.
Frontend:
JavaScript (React, Vue, or Angular) for developing the client side with an intuitive interface.
Integration with Kafka for handling events between microservices (for example, notifications about order status).
Swagger for API documentation.
WebSockets for real-time updates about order status and product availability.
4. Database design (see the feature for the up-to-date tables and data, as new fields may be added)
It is necessary to design the table structure for PostgreSQL:
Users table:
• user_id (primary key)
• name
• email
• password_hash
Products table:
• product_id (primary key)
• name
• description
• stock_quantity (quantity in stock)
Orders table:
• order_id (primary key)
• user_id (foreign key)
• product_id (foreign key)
• quantity
• order_status (for example, “pending”, “completed”, “canceled”)
Transactions table (for monitoring operations):
• transaction_id (primary key)
• user_id (foreign key)
• product_id (foreign key)
• action (for example, “add product”, “delete product”, “create order”)
5. Authentication implementation
Implement registration and login using JWT (JSON Web Token) so that users can only work with their own data. This will make it possible to restrict access at the API level.
6. Microservice development
Development of each microservice separately:
• Users: CRUD operations on users, registration, authentication.
• Products: adding, deleting, modifying product information, managing stock.
• Orders: creating, fulfilling, canceling orders.
Communication via Kafka will allow passing event messages between services, for example, when an order status changes.
7. Logging and monitoring via the ELK stack
Set up logging of all actions in the system so that it is possible to track who performed which actions. In case of problems with order fulfillment, logging via the ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) will help find the cause.
8. Creating and documenting the API
Use Swagger to document all APIs, so that developers have the ability to easily test and explore the available routes.
9. Frontend implementation
Develop the client side so that users can interact with the system through a browser. This will be the interface for managing products, orders, and their statuses.

doc_6387ce98.content:
Changes in the authorization process
A new tokens table has been added to the authorization microservice for storing the token.


The token is assigned to a specific user upon authorization in the system (via user_id, which corresponds to the user’s id in the system) and is subsequently used to authorize the user in the system when performing subsequent operations.
Two types of tokens are used for user operation:
access_token — a short-lived token that is active for 30 minutes
refresh_token — a long-lived token that is active for 7 days.
When the “Remember me” button is clicked at the moment of authorization in the system, the refresh token is written to a cookie.
When the access_token expires, but a refresh_token is present in the cookie, the access_token is automatically regenerated and written to the DB.
If there is no refresh_token, the user must regenerate the access_token by re-authorizing in the system.
Only one record can exist in the database for a single user.

doc_50147a21.content:
Technical requirements
User registration and management in the system with access rights differentiation
Description:
As a user, I want to be able to register in the system, log in using a token, and see my data. As a super-admin, I want to manage all users and assign them roles to differentiate access rights.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. User registration
• The user must provide the following data:
Name:
• Required field.
• Minimum 3 characters, maximum 50 characters.
Email:
• Required field.
• Must be valid and unique.
• Format: standard email format, validated using regular expressions.
Password:
• Required field.
• Minimum 8 characters.
• Must be hashed before being saved to the database.
• If the email already exists in the system, an error message must be shown to the user.
• The password must be hashed using the bcrypt algorithm.
• After successful registration, the user is created as a limited user (limited_user) in the PostgreSQL database, and the limited_user role is assigned to them.

2. User authorization
• The user must be able to log in by entering a valid email and password.
• On successful login, the system returns:
Access Token implemented using the JWT technology (link to a short article about this technology):ссылка на небольшую статью о данной технологии
• Token expiration time — 30 minutes.
• If the email or password is incorrect, an authorization error message is returned.
• The token must remain valid for 30 minutes from the moment of login.

3. Viewing user data
• A regular user can get information only about themselves.
• When making a request to /users/, the user sees only their own data, namely:
• User ID (UUID).
• User name (3–50 characters).
• User email.
• Role: user.
• The super-admin can see the list of all users in the system.

4. Promotion to super-admin (for super-admin)
• The super-admin must be able to promote other users to the super-admin role.
• The is_superadmin field changes from False to True.
• Promotion can only be performed for users who are already registered.
• A user cannot promote themselves to super-admin.
• Promotion is performed via an API request specifying the user ID (UUID)

Requirements for access:
• The user must be a super-administrator.
• Bearer token (JWT) is passed via the Authorization header.

5. User management (for super-admin)
• The super-admin must be able to view the list of all registered users in the system.
• The list contains:
• User ID (UUID).
• User name (3–50 characters).
• User email.
• User role: superadmin or user.

Requirements for access:
• The user must be a super-administrator.
• Bearer token (JWT) is passed via the Authorization header.
6. Editing a user
• The super-admin must be able to edit their own data and the data of any existing users.
• Fields available for editing:
• Email: valid email.
• User name: a string from 3 to 50 characters long.
• Changes can only be made for users who are already registered.
• A user cannot edit their own data through this method.

Requirements for access:
• The user must be a super-administrator.
• Bearer token (JWT) is passed via the Authorization header.

Notes:
• If a user with the specified ID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
• If the current user does not have sufficient permissions, a 403 Forbidden error is returned.
• The API response must contain a success message about the update and the fields of the edited user: id, name, email

7. Deleting a user
• The super-admin must be able to delete registered users.
• Deletion can only be performed for users who are already registered.
• A user cannot delete themselves through this method.
• User deletion is performed by user_id (the id in the DB)

Requirements for access:
• The user must be a super-administrator.
• Bearer token (JWT) is passed via the Authorization header.

Notes:
• If a user with the specified ID is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
• If the current user does not have sufficient permissions, a 403 Forbidden error is returned.
• The API response must contain a message about successful user deletion

Technical details:
User name (name):
• A string from 3 to 50 characters long.
• DB constraint: NOT NULL and length from 3 to 50 characters.
Email:
• Unique field.
• Valid email format (validated using a regular expression).
• DB constraint: UNIQUE and NOT NULL.
Password:
• A string (bcrypt hash).
• Minimum 8 characters.
• DB constraint: NOT NULL.
User ID (id):
• Type: UUID.
• Generated automatically when the user is created.
• DB constraint: UNIQUE, NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY.
User role (is_superadmin):
• Boolean value: True or False.
• Default: False (regular user).

doc_c39ee3b6.content:
Technical requirements for the Chat_service microservice
Internal chat functionality
Description:
As a user, I want to be able to:
• Create chats (administrator only).
• Add users to chats (administrator only).
• View the list of my chats.
• Receive messages from chats in which I participate.
• Send messages to chats.
• Use WebSocket for real-time communication.

Access to this data is performed via an authorization token (Bearer JWT), with access rights differentiation.

1. Chat management
1.1 Creating a chat
• Only a user with the administrator role (is_superadmin = True) can create a chat.
• The chat must contain at least two participants.
• When a chat is created, participants are specified who are immediately added to it.
• A user who is not an administrator cannot create a chat; a 403 Requires admin access error is returned.

Data that the user must provide:
Name:
• Required field.
• Maximum length 100 characters.
• No requirements for characters or uniqueness.

Chat type (is_group):
• Required field.
• Accepts the values true (group chat) or false (direct chat).

List of participants (participants):
• Required field.
• At least two participants.
• Must be passed as a list of UUIDs.
1.2 Getting the list of user’s chats
• The user must be able to get the list of all chats in which they participate.
• Returned fields:
• Chat ID.
• Chat name.
• Chat type.
• Creation date.
• List of participants.
• If the user is not authorized, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.
• If the user has no chats, an empty list is returned.
1.3 Getting messages in a chat
• The user must be able to get the list of messages in a chat in which they are a participant.
• Returned fields:
• Message ID.
• Chat ID.
• Sender ID.
• Message text.
• Sent date.
• If the user is not authorized, a 401 Unauthorized error is returned.
• If the chat is not found, a 404 Not Found error is returned.
• If the user is not a participant of the chat, a 403 Forbidden error is returned.
1.4 Adding a user to a chat
• Only an administrator (is_superadmin = True) can add a user to a chat.
• It is not allowed to add a user who is already in the chat; a 422 User already in chat error must be returned.
• If the user is not an administrator, a 403 Forbidden error is returned.
• If the chat is not found, a 404 Not found error is returned.

Data that the administrator must provide:
Chat ID (chat_id):
• Required field.
• Must be a valid UUID.

User ID (user_id):
• Required field.
• Must be a valid UUID.

2. Message management
2.1 Sending a message to a chat
• The user must be able to send a message to a chat in which they are a participant.
• If the user is not a member of the chat, a 403 Forbidden error must be returned.
• If the chat is not found, a 404 Chat not found error is returned.

Data that the user must provide:
Chat ID (chat_id):
• Required field.
• Must be a valid UUID.

Message text (content):
• Required field.
• Maximum length 1000 characters.

3. WebSockets (WebSocket API)
3.1 Description
• WebSocket allows users to exchange messages in real time.
• On connection, the client receives the last 50 messages from the chat, if any exist.
• A message sent via WebSocket is automatically broadcast to all chat participants.
3.2 Establishing the connection
• The user must connect to the address: ws://{your_host}:{your_port}/ws/{chat_id}/{user_id}
• If the user is not a member of the chat, the connection is terminated with a 403 User is not a participant of this chat error.
• If an incorrect UUID format is passed, the connection is terminated with a 400 Invalid UUID format error.
3.3 Sending a message via WebSocket
• After a successful connection, the user can send a JSON object with a message in the format {"content": “Message text"}.
• The message is immediately saved to the database, cached in Redis, and passed through Kafka.
• All chat participants receive the message in real time.

4. Kafka and Redis logic
4.1 Kafka logic
• Kafka is used to process notifications about new messages in chats.
• Every time a user sends a message, it is sent to the Kafka topic chat_notifications.
• The Kafka Consumer listens to this topic and processes incoming messages.

Process of sending a message via Kafka:
1. The user sends a message via WebSocket.
2. The server processes the message and saves it to the database.
3. The message is sent to the Kafka topic chat_notifications.
4. The Kafka Consumer listens to the topic and registers new messages.

4.2 Redis logic
• Redis is used to cache the last 50 messages in a chat.
• Each new message is added to the chat_history:{chat_id} list in Redis.
• When a user connects to the WebSocket, the server loads the last 50 messages from Redis.
• Redis helps reduce the number of requests to the database and speed up access to chat history.

Process of caching messages in Redis:
1. The user connects to the WebSocket.
2. The server loads the last 50 chat messages from Redis.
3. The user sends a new message to the chat.
4. The message is added to the database and saved in Redis.
5. On the next WebSocket connection, Redis will provide fast access to the message history.

doc_db8f83cc.content:
Technical requirements for the Orders_service microservice
Order management in the system

Description:
As a user, I want to be able to manage shipping orders from warehouses: create new orders, view the details of existing orders, and get the list of all my orders. Access to this data is performed via an authorization token with access rights differentiation for authorized users. The super-admin can get information about all orders in the system.

1. Order management
1.1 Creating an order
• The user must be able to create a new order.
• When creating an order, the availability of the selected products and their sufficient quantity in the warehouse is checked.
• If the product does not exist or there is not enough of it in the warehouse, a “Product not available or insufficient quantity” message is returned in Kafka.

Data that the user must provide:
order_items (list of products in the order):
• product_id:
• Required field.
• Must be a valid UUID of an existing product.
• quantity:
• Required field.
• An integer greater than 0.
• warehouse_id:
• Required field.
• Must be a valid UUID of an existing warehouse that has the product.

Constraints:
• product_id must correspond to an existing product in the database.
• quantity cannot exceed the available quantity of the product in the warehouse.
• The user must be authorized.

1.2 Getting the list of orders
• The user must be able to get the list of all their orders.
Returned fields:
• order_id: order UUID.
• user_id: user UUID.
• status: order status (pending, completed, cancelled).
• created_at: date and time of order creation.
• updated_at: date and time of the last order update.
• order_items: list of products in the order, including:
• product_id: product UUID.
• warehouse_id: product UUID.
• quantity: quantity of the product.
• price_at_order: price of the product at the time of the order.

Criteria:
• The user must be authorized.
• If there are no orders, an empty list is returned.
• If the user is not authorized, an Unauthorized message is returned.
• If the user is a Super-admin, they get the list of all orders in the system

1.3 Getting an order by ID
• The user must be able to get information about a specific order by its order_id.
• The returned fields are the same as in item 1.2.
• If the order with the specified order_id is not found or does not belong to the user, a Not Found message is returned.
• If the user is not authorized, an Unauthorized message is returned.
• If the user is a Super-admin, they get information about the order regardless of who created it in the system

1.4 Canceling an order
• The user can cancel an order with the pending status.
• When the order is canceled, its status changes to cancelled.
• If the order has already been processed (completed status) or has already been canceled, a “Cannot cancel this order” message is returned.
• If the order with the specified order_id is not found or does not belong to the user, a Not Found message is returned.

1.5 Updating the order status
• Updating the order status is performed automatically by the system based on events (for example, payment confirmation, order shipment, etc.).
• The user cannot directly change the order status.

2. Order statuses
pending:
• The order has been created and is awaiting processing.
completed:
• The order has been processed and completed.
cancelled:
• The order has been canceled by the user or the system.

3. Technical requirements
• Data validation:
• All input data must be validated against the requirements (data type, value range, UUID format, etc.).
• Error handling:
• Error messages must be informative.
• Logging:
• Requests and errors must be logged for subsequent analysis and debugging.
